records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for the Nutfah (sperm), Alaqah (clinging clot), Mudghah (embryonic lump), Bone, and Fetus
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5321 - In the narration by Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub from Ali ibn Riʾab from Abu Ubaydah from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a woman who drank medicine while she was pregnant to abort her child and then miscarried.
Imam (as) said: "If the fetus had developed bones covered with flesh and its hearing and sight had been formed, then she must pay the full blood money (diyyah) and hand it over to the child's father."
Imam (as) further said: "If it was still in the stage of a clot (Alaqah) or a piece of flesh (muḍghah), then she must pay forty dinars or provide a ghurra (compensation), handing it over to the father."
I asked: "Does she inherit anything from her child's blood money?"
Imam (as) replied: "No, because she killed the child."
records.arabicText: 5321 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اِمْرَأَةٍ شَرِبَتْ دَوَاءً وَ هِيَ حَامِلٌ لِتَطْرَحَ وَلَدَهَا فَأَلْقَتْ وَلَدَهَا قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ عَظْمٌ قَدْ نَبَتَ عَلَيْهِ اَللَّحْمُ وَ شُقَّ لَهُ اَلسَّمْعُ وَ اَلْبَصَرُ فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهَا دِيَةً تُسَلِّمُهَا إِلَى أَبِيهِ" قَالَ "وَ إِنْ كَانَ عَلَقَةً أَوْ مُضْغَةً فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهَا أَرْبَعِينَ دِينَاراً أَوْ غُرَّةً تُسَلِّمُهَا إِلَى أَبِيهِ" قُلْتُ فَهِيَ لاَ تَرِثُ مِنْ وَلَدِهَا مِنْ دِيَتِهِ قَالَ "لاَ لِأَنَّهَا قَتَلَتْهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5321 - In the narration by Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub from Ali ibn Riʾab from Abu Ubaydah from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a woman who drank medicine while she was pregnant to abort her child and then miscarried.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "If the fetus had developed bones covered with flesh and its hearing and sight had been formed, then she must pay the full blood money (diyyah) and hand it over to the child's father."
Imam (as) further said: "If it was still in the stage of a clot (Alaqah) or a piece of flesh (muḍghah), then she must pay forty dinars or provide a ghurra (compensation), handing it over to the father."
I asked: "Does she inherit anything from her child's blood money?"
Imam (as) replied: "No, because she killed the child."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for the Nutfah (sperm), Alaqah (clinging clot), Mudghah (embryonic lump), Bone, and Fetus
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5322 - In the narration by Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub from Nuaym ibn Ibrahim from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who killed the fetus of a female slave belonging to a group of people while it was still in her womb.
Imam (as) said: "If the fetus died in her womb after he struck her, then he must pay half of one-tenth of the value of the female slave. However, if he struck her and she miscarried the fetus alive and then it died, he must pay one-tenth of the value of the female slave."
records.arabicText: 5322 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ نُعَيْمِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ جَنِينَ أَمَةٍ لِقَوْمٍ فِي بَطْنِهَا فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ مَاتَ فِي بَطْنِهَا بَعْدَ مَا ضَرَبَهَا فَعَلَيْهِ نِصْفُ عُشْرِ قِيمَةِ اَلْأَمَةِ وَ إِنْ ضَرَبَهَا فَأَلْقَتْهُ حَيّاً فَمَاتَ فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهِ عُشْرَ قِيمَةِ اَلْأَمَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5322 - In the narration by Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub from Nuaym ibn Ibrahim from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who killed the fetus of a female slave belonging to a group of people while it was still in her womb.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If the fetus died in her womb after he struck her, then he must pay half of one-tenth of the value of the female slave. However, if he struck her and she miscarried the fetus alive and then it died, he must pay one-tenth of the value of the female slave."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for the Nutfah (sperm), Alaqah (clinging clot), Mudghah (embryonic lump), Bone, and Fetus
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5323 - Samaah asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who struck his pregnant daughter, causing her to miscarry a stillborn child. The woman's husband sought legal action against her father.
The woman said to her husband, "If this fetus has a diyyah (blood money), and I am entitled to an inheritance from it, then I give my share of the inheritance to my father."
Imam (as) responded: "It is permissible for her father to receive what she has gifted to him."
records.arabicText: 5323 - وَ سَأَلَ سَمَاعَةُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ ضَرَبَ اِبْنَتَهُ وَ هِيَ حُبْلَى فَأَسْقَطَتْ سِقْطاً مَيِّتاً فَاسْتَعْدَى زَوْجُ اَلْمَرْأَةِ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالَتِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ لِزَوْجِهَا إِنْ كَانَ لِهَذَا اَلسِّقْطِ دِيَةٌ وَ لِي مِنْهُ مِيرَاثٌ فَإِنَّ مِيرَاثِي مِنْهُ لِأَبِي قَالَ "يَجُوزُ لِأَبِيهَا مَا وَهَبَتْ لَهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5323 - Samaah asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who struck his pregnant daughter, causing her to miscarry a stillborn child. The woman's husband sought legal action against her father.
The woman said to her husband, "If this fetus has a diyyah (blood money), and I am entitled to an inheritance from it, then I give my share of the inheritance to my father."
Imam (as) responded:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is permissible for her father to receive what she has gifted to him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for the Nutfah (sperm), Alaqah (clinging clot), Mudghah (embryonic lump), Bone, and Fetus
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5324 - Al-Husayn ibn Saʿid narrated from Muhammad ibn Al-Fuḍayl who said:
I asked Abu Al-Hasan (as) about a thief who entered upon a pregnant woman, assaulted her, and caused her to miscarry. The woman then attacked the thief and killed him.
Imam (as) replied: "The blood of the thief is not avenged (yutallu), but the diyyah (blood money) for her miscarried fetus is due upon her."
records.arabicText: 5324 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ لِصٍّ دَخَلَ عَلَى اِمْرَأَةٍ حُبْلَى فَوَقَعَ عَلَيْهَا فَأَلْقَتْ مَا فِي بَطْنِهَا فَوَثَبَتْ عَلَيْهِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ فَقَتَلَتْهُ قَالَ "يُطَلُّ دَمُ اَللِّصِّ وَ عَلَى اَلْمَقْتُولِ دِيَةُ سَخْلَتِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5324 - Al-Husayn ibn Saʿid narrated from Muhammad ibn Al-Fuḍayl who said:
I asked Abu Al-Hasan (as) about a thief who entered upon a pregnant woman, assaulted her, and caused her to miscarry. The woman then attacked the thief and killed him.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The blood of the thief is not avenged (yutallu), but the diyyah (blood money) for her miscarried fetus is due upon her."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory When a Muslim Man is in the Land of Polytheism and is Killed by Muslims, and Then the Imam Becomes Aware of it
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5325 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from some of his companions from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a Muslim man who was in the land of the disbelievers and was mistakenly killed by the Muslims. Later, the Imam became aware of it.
Imam (as) said: "A believing slave must be freed in his place, and this is in accordance with the saying of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic:
'But if he belonged to a people hostile to you and he was a believer, then the freeing of a believing slave [is required]' (Surah An-Nisa 4:92).
records.arabicText: 5325 - رَوَى اِبْنُ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ مُسْلِمٍ كَانَ فِي أَرْضِ اَلشِّرْكِ فَقَتَلَهُ اَلْمُسْلِمُونَ ثُمَّ عَلِمَ بِهِ اَلْإِمَامُ بَعْدُ فَقَالَ "يُعْتِقُ مَكَانَهُ رَقَبَةً مُؤْمِنَةً وَ ذَلِكَ قَوْلُ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ۝ فَإِنْ كٰانَ مِنْ قَوْمٍ عَدُوٍّ لَكُمْ وَ هُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَتَحْرِيرُ رَقَبَةٍ مُؤْمِنَةٍ ۝ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5325 - Ibn Abi Umayr narrated from some of his companions from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a Muslim man who was in the land of the disbelievers and was mistakenly killed by the Muslims. Later, the Imam became aware of it.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "A believing slave must be freed in his place, and this is in accordance with the saying of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic:
'But if he belonged to a people hostile to you and he was a believer, then the freeing of a believing slave [is required]' (Surah An-Nisa 4:92).

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Steps on a man's Abdomen Causing Him to Soil His Clothes
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5326 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni, it is reported that a man was brought to Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) who had trampled on another man's stomach until he defecated in his clothes. Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled:
"His stomach should be trampled until he defecates as the victim did, or he must pay one-third of the blood money (diyyah)."
records.arabicText: 5326 - فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ : "أَنَّ رَجُلاً رُفِعَ إِلَى عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ قَدْ دَاسَ بَطْنَ رَجُلٍ حَتَّى أَحْدَثَ فِي ثِيَابِهِ فَقَضَى عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَلَيْهِ "أَنْ يُدَاسَ بَطْنُهُ حَتَّى يُحْدِثَ كَمَا أَحْدَثَ أَوْ يَغْرَمَ ثُلُثَ اَلدِّيَةِ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5326 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni, it is reported that a man was brought to Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) who had trampled on another man's stomach until he defecated in his clothes. Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "His stomach should be trampled until he defecates as the victim did, or he must pay one-third of the blood money (diyyah)."

records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Who Transgresses in Marrying a Woman and Persists Until She Dies
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5327 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Al-Harith ibn Muhammad from Zayd from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a man who had intercourse with his wife in her rear and persisted until she died from it.
Imam (as) said: "He must pay the full blood money (diyyah)."
records.arabicText: 5327 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنِ اَلْحَارِثِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ زَيْدٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ نَكَحَ اِمْرَأَتَهُ فِي دُبُرِهَا فَأَلَحَّ عَلَيْهَا حَتَّى مَاتَتْ مِنْ ذَلِكَ قَالَ "عَلَيْهِ اَلدِّيَةُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5327 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Al-Harith ibn Muhammad from Zayd from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a man who had intercourse with his wife in her rear and persisted until she died from it.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He must pay the full blood money (diyyah)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for the Tongue of a Mute Person
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5328 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from Abu Baṣir from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that someone from the family of Zurarah asked Imam (as) about a man who cut off the tongue of a mute person.
Imam (as) said: "If his mother gave birth to him mute, then the full blood money (diyyah) is due. But if his tongue was lost due to an illness or an affliction after he had been able to speak, then the one who cut it off must pay one-third of the diyyah of the tongue."
records.arabicText: 5328 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلَهُ بَعْضُ آلِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَطَعَ لِسَانَ رَجُلٍ أَخْرَسَ فَقَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ وَلَدَتْهُ أُمُّهُ وَ هُوَ أَخْرَسُ فَعَلَيْهِ اَلدِّيَةُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ لِسَانُهُ ذَهَبَ بِوَجَعٍ أَوْ آفَةٍ بَعْدَ مَا كَانَ يَتَكَلَّمُ فَإِنَّ عَلَى اَلَّذِي قَطَعَ ثُلُثَ دِيَةِ لِسَانِهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5328 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim from Abu Baṣir from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) that someone from the family of Zurarah asked Imam (as) about a man who cut off the tongue of a mute person.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "If his mother gave birth to him mute, then the full blood money (diyyah) is due. But if his tongue was lost due to an illness or an affliction after he had been able to speak, then the one who cut it off must pay one-third of the diyyah of the tongue."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory in Cases of ifda' (causing Reproductive rupture)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: The Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled in the case of a woman who was harmed through Ifdaʾ (a severe injury during intercourse that causes damage) that full blood money (diyyah) is due.
records.arabicText: قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي اِمْرَأَةٍ أُفْضِيَتْ بِالدِّيَةِ".

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory in Cases of ifda' (causing Reproductive rupture)
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5329 - In Nawadir Al-Hikmah, it is narrated that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said: In the case of a man whose wife caused harm (Ifdaʾ) to his bondwoman with her hand, the ruling was that the bondwoman should be appraised at her full value when she was healthy and then at her value after being harmed. The wife must pay the difference between the value when she was healthy and the value after the defect. Furthermore, the wife was compelled to keep the bondwoman in her possession because she was no longer suitable for men.
records.arabicText: 5329 - وَ فِي نَوَادِرِ اَلْحِكْمَةِ أَنَّ اَلصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "فِي رَجُلٍ أَفْضَتِ اِمْرَأَتُهُ جَارِيَتَهُ بِيَدِهَا فَقَضَى أَنْ تُقَوَّمَ قِيمَةً وَ هِيَ صَحِيحَةٌ وَ قِيمَةً وَ هِيَ مُفْضَاةٌ فَيُغْرِمَهَا مَا بَيْنَ اَلصِّحَّةِ وَ اَلْعَيْبِ وَ أَجْبَرَهَا عَلَى إِمْسَاكِهَا لِأَنَّهَا لاَ تَصْلُحُ لِلرِّجَالِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5329 - In Nawadir Al-Hikmah, it is narrated that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: In the case of a man whose wife caused harm (Ifdaʾ) to his bondwoman with her hand, the ruling was that the bondwoman should be appraised at her full value when she was healthy and then at her value after being harmed. The wife must pay the difference between the value when she was healthy and the value after the defect. Furthermore, the wife was compelled to keep the bondwoman in her possession because she was no longer suitable for men.

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Upon Whom Hot Water is Poured on His Head, Causing His Hair to Fall Out
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5330 - It is narrated from Ja'far ibn Bashir, from Hisham ibn Salim, from Sulayman ibn Khalid, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who poured hot water on another man's head, causing his hair to fall out and never grow back.
Imam (as) said: "He must pay the full blood money (diyyah)."
records.arabicText: 5330 - رَوَى جَعْفَرُ بْنُ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ سُلَيْمَانَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ صَبَّ مَاءً حَارّاً عَلَى رَأْسِ رَجُلٍ فَامْتَعَطَ شَعْرُهُ فَلاَ يَنْبُتُ أَبَداً قَالَ "عَلَيْهِ اَلدِّيَةُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5330 - It is narrated from Ja'far ibn Bashir, from Hisham ibn Salim, from Sulayman ibn Khalid, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who poured hot water on another man's head, causing his hair to fall out and never grow back.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He must pay the full blood money (diyyah)."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Upon Whom Hot Water is Poured on His Head, Causing His Hair to Fall Out
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5331 - It is narrated from Salamah ibn Tammam that a man poured a pot of broth over another man's head, causing his hair to fall out. They disputed this matter before Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), who delayed the judgment for a year. When the man's hair did not grow back, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled that the perpetrator must pay the full blood money (diyyah).
records.arabicText: 5331 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ سَلَمَةَ بْنِ تَمَّامٍ قَالَ: أَهْرَاقَ رَجُلٌ عَلَى رَأْسِ رَجُلٍ قِدْراً فِيهَا مَرَقٌ فَذَهَبَ شَعْرُهُ فَاخْتَصَمُوا فِي ذَلِكَ إِلَى عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَأَجَّلَهُ سَنَةً فَلَمْ يَنْبُتْ شَعْرُهُ فَقَضَى عَلَيْهِ بِالدِّيَةِ.

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory in the Case of a Shaved Beard
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5332 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni, it is reported that Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled that if a man's beard was shaved off and it did not grow back, the full blood money (diyyah) must be paid. However, if the beard grew back, only one-third of the diyyah was required.
records.arabicText: 5332 - فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَضَى فِي اَللِّحْيَةِ إِذَا حُلِقَتْ فَلَمْ تَنْبُتْ بِالدِّيَةِ كَامِلَةً فَإِذَا نَبَتَتْ فَثُلُثُ اَلدِّيَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5332 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni, it is reported that
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ruled that if a man's beard was shaved off and it did not grow back, the full blood money (diyyah) must be paid. However, if the beard grew back, only one-third of the diyyah was required.

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Cuts His wife's Private Part
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5333 - Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Indeed, in the book of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), it is written: 'If a man were to cut off the private part of his wife, I would obligate him to pay her full blood money (diyyah). If he does not pay her the diyyah, I would cut off his private part for her if she demands it.'"
records.arabicText: 5333 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ سَيَابَةَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ "إِنَّ فِي كِتَابِ عَلِيٍّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لَوْ أَنَّ رَجُلاً قَطَعَ فَرْجَ اِمْرَأَتِهِ لَأُغْرِمَنَّهُ لَهَا دِيَتَهَا فَإِنْ لَمْ يُؤَدِّ إِلَيْهَا اَلدِّيَةَ قَطَعْتُ لَهَا فَرْجَهُ إِنْ طَلَبَتْ ذَلِكَ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5333 - Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Indeed, in the book of Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as), it is written:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'If a man were to cut off the private part of his wife, I would obligate him to pay her full blood money (diyyah). If he does not pay her the diyyah, I would cut off his private part for her if she demands it.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Kicks a Woman in Her Private Part, and She Claims That She No Longer Menstruates
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5334 - Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about a man who kicked a woman in her private area, and she claimed that her menstruation had stopped while it was previously regular.
Imam (as) said: "She should be observed for one year. If her menstruation returns, then nothing is required. But if it does not return, the man must pay one-third of her blood money (diyyah) for the damage to her menstruation and the harm caused to her womb."
records.arabicText: 5334 - رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ رِجَالِهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ رَكَلَ اِمْرَأَةً فِي فَرْجِهَا فَزَعَمَتْ أَنَّهَا لاَ تَحِيضُ وَ كَانَ طَمْثُهَا مُسْتَقِيماً قَالَ "يُتَرَبَّصُ بِهَا سَنَةً فَإِنْ رَجَعَ إِلَيْهَا اَلطَّمْثُ وَ إِلاَّ غُرِّمَ اَلرَّجُلُ ثُلُثَ دِيَتِهَا لِفَسَادِ طَمْثِهَا وَ عَقْرِ رَحِمِهَا".

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Kicks a Woman in Her Private Part, and She Claims That She No Longer Menstruates
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5335 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Hisham ibn Salim, from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as):
"What is your ruling regarding a man who struck a young woman on her abdomen, causing damage to her womb and disrupting her menstruation? She reported that her menstruation had ceased due to this injury, whereas it had previously been regular."
Imam (as) said: "She should be observed for one year. If her womb recovers and her menstruation returns to normal, then there is no liability. But if it does not return, she is to take an oath, and the assailant must pay one-third of her diyyah (blood money) for the damage to her womb and the cessation of her menstruation."
records.arabicText: 5335 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَا تَرَى فِي رَجُلٍ ضَرَبَ اِمْرَأَةً شَابَّةً عَلَى بَطْنِهَا فَعَقَرَ رَحِمَهَا وَ أَفْسَدَ طَمْثَهَا وَ ذَكَرَتْ أَنَّهُ قَدِ اِرْتَفَعَ طَمْثُهَا عَنْهَا لِذَلِكَ وَ قَدْ كَانَ طَمْثُهَا مُسْتَقِيماً قَالَ "يُنْتَظَرُ بِهَا سَنَةً فَإِنْ صَلَحَ رَحِمُهَا وَ عَادَ طَمْثُهَا إِلَى مَا كَانَ وَ إِلاَّ اُسْتُحْلِفَتْ وَ أُغْرِمَ ضَارِبُهَا ثُلُثَ دِيَتِهَا لِفَسَادِ رَحِمِهَا وَ اِرْتِفَاعِ طَمْثِهَا".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for the Joints of the Fingers
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5336 - In a narration from Al-Sakuni, it is reported that Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled that for every joint in the fingers, the compensation (diyyah) would be one-third of the full blood money of that finger, except for the thumb.
For the thumb, he ruled that the compensation for its joint would be half of its total blood money because it has two joints.
records.arabicText: 5336 - فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ : "أَنَّ أَمِيرَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ كَانَ يَقْضِي فِي كُلِّ مَفْصِلٍ مِنَ اَلْأَصَابِعِ بِثُلُثِ عَقْلِ تِلْكَ اَلْأَصَابِعِ إِلاَّ اَلْإِبْهَامَ فَإِنَّهُ كَانَ يَقْضِي فِي مَفْصِلِهَا بِنِصْفِ عَقْلِ تِلْكَ اَلْإِبْهَامِ لِأَنَّ لَهَا مَفْصِلَيْنِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5336 - In a narration from Al-Sakuni, it is reported that
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled that for every joint in the fingers, the compensation (diyyah) would be one-third of the full blood money of that finger, except for the thumb.
For the thumb, he ruled that the compensation for its joint would be half of its total blood money because it has two joints.

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for the Testicles
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5337 - In a narration from Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Yahya ibn Imran Al-Ash'ari through Muhammad ibn Harun from Abu Yahya Al-Wasiti, who raised it to Abu Abdullah (as), it is reported that Imam (as) said: "A child is conceived from the left testicle, so if it is severed, the compensation (diyyah) is two-thirds of the full blood money, and for the right testicle, it is one-third of the blood money."
records.arabicText: 5337 - فِي رِوَايَةِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ يَحْيَى بْنِ عِمْرَانَ اَلْأَشْعَرِيِّ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ هَارُونَ عَنْ أَبِي يَحْيَى اَلْوَاسِطِيِّ رَفَعَهُ إِلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "اَلْوَلَدُ يَكُونُ مِنَ اَلْبَيْضَةِ اَلْيُسْرَى فَإِذَا قُطِعَتْ فَفِيهَا ثُلُثَا اَلدِّيَةِ وَ فِي اَلْيُمْنَى ثُلُثُ اَلدِّيَةِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5337 - In a narration from Muhammad ibn Ahmad ibn Yahya ibn Imran Al-Ash'ari through Muhammad ibn Harun from Abu Yahya Al-Wasiti, who raised it to Abu Abdullah (as), it is reported that
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "A child is conceived from the left testicle, so if it is severed, the compensation (diyyah) is two-thirds of the full blood money, and for the right testicle, it is one-third of the blood money."

records.chapter: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding Four Persons Who Kill a Man; a Slave, a Free Man, a Free Woman, and a Mukatab.
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5338 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about four individuals who killed a man: one was a slave, one was a free man, one was a free woman, and one was a mukatab (a slave who had entered into a contract for his freedom and had paid half of it).
Imam (as) said: "The compensation (diyyah) is to be divided as follows:
the free man must pay one-fourth of the diyyah,
the free woman must pay one-fourth of the diyyah,
and regarding the slave, his master is given the choice - if he wills, he can pay on his behalf, and if he wills, he can surrender him entirely, and the slave's family bears no responsibility.
As for the mukatab, half of one-fourth of the diyyah is taken from his own wealth, and the other half of that one-fourth is taken from those who entered into the contract with him, because half of him has been freed."
records.arabicText: 5338 - سُئِلَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ أَرْبَعَةِ أَنْفُسٍ قَتَلُوا رَجُلاً مَمْلُوكٍ وَ حُرٍّ وَ حُرَّةٍ وَ مُكَاتَبٍ قَدْ أَدَّى نِصْفَ مُكَاتَبَتِهِ فَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "عَلَيْهِمُ اَلدِّيَةُ عَلَى اَلْحُرِّ رُبُعُ اَلدِّيَةِ وَ عَلَى اَلْحُرَّةِ رُبُعُ اَلدِّيَةِ وَ عَلَى اَلْمَمْلُوكِ أَنْ يُخَيَّرَ مَوْلاَهُ فَإِنْ شَاءَ أَدَّى عَنْهُ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ دَفَعَهُ بِرُمَّتِهِ وَ لاَ يَغْرَمُ أَهْلُهُ شَيْئاً وَ عَلَى اَلْمُكَاتَبِ فِي مَالِهِ نِصْفُ اَلرُّبُعِ وَ عَلَى اَلَّذِينَ كَاتَبُوهُ نِصْفُ اَلرُّبُعِ فَذَلِكَ اَلرُّبُعُ لِأَنَّهُ قَدْ عَتَقَ نِصْفُهُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5338 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) was asked about four individuals who killed a man: one was a slave, one was a free man, one was a free woman, and one was a mukatab (a slave who had entered into a contract for his freedom and had paid half of it).
Imam (as) said: "The compensation (diyyah) is to be divided as follows:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
the free man must pay one-fourth of the diyyah,
the free woman must pay one-fourth of the diyyah,
and regarding the slave, his master is given the choice - if he wills, he can pay on his behalf, and if he wills, he can surrender him entirely, and the slave's family bears no responsibility.
As for the mukatab, half of one-fourth of the diyyah is taken from his own wealth, and the other half of that one-fourth is taken from those who entered into the contract with him, because half of him has been freed."

records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Tortures His Slave Until He Dies
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5339 - In a narration from Al-Sakuni, it is reported that a man was brought before Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) who had tortured his slave until the slave died.
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ordered that the man be lashed one hundred times as a form of punishment, imprisoned him, and required him to pay the value of the slave, which was then given in charity.
records.arabicText: 5339 - فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رُفِعَ إِلَيْهِ رَجُلٌ عَذَّبَ عَبْدَهُ حَتَّى مَاتَ فَضَرَبَهُ مِائَةً نَكَالاً وَ حَبَسَهُ وَ غَرَّمَهُ قِيمَةَ اَلْعَبْدِ وَ تَصَدَّقَ بِهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5339 - In a narration from Al-Sakuni, it is reported that
records.thaqalaynMatn:
a man was brought before Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) who had tortured his slave until the slave died.
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ordered that the man be lashed one hundred times as a form of punishment, imprisoned him, and required him to pay the value of the slave, which was then given in charity.

records.chapter: Chapter on the Blood Money for the Child of Adultery
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5340 - In a narration from Ja'far ibn Bashir through some of his transmitters, it is reported that someone asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the blood money (diya) of a child born from adultery.
Imam (as) replied: "Eight hundred dirhams, similar to the diyyah of a Jew, a Christian, and a Magian."
records.arabicText: 5340 - فِي رِوَايَةِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ رِجَالِهِ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ دِيَةِ وَلَدِ اَلزِّنَا قَالَ "ثَمَانُمِائَةِ دِرْهَمٍ مِثْلُ دِيَةِ اَلْيَهُودِيِّ وَ اَلنَّصْرَانِيِّ وَ اَلْمَجُوسِيِّ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5340 - In a narration from Ja'far ibn Bashir through some of his transmitters, it is reported that someone asked Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about the blood money (diya) of a child born from adultery.
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Eight hundred dirhams, similar to the diyyah of a Jew, a Christian, and a Magian."

records.chapter: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5341 - Zurah and Uthman ibn Isa narrated from Samaah, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who digs a well in his house or on his land.
Imam (as) said: "If he digs it within his own property, there is no liability upon him. But if he digs it in a public road or in a place that does not belong to him, then he is liable for anything that falls into it."
records.arabicText: 5341 - رَوَى زُرْعَةُ وَ عُثْمَانُ بْنُ عِيسَى عَنْ سَمَاعَةَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَحْفِرَ اَلْبِئْرَ فِي دَارِهِ أَوْ فِي أَرْضِهِ فَقَالَ "أَمَّا مَا حَفَرَ فِي مِلْكِهِ فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ ضَمَانٌ وَ أَمَّا مَا حَفَرَ فِي اَلطَّرِيقِ أَوْ فِي غَيْرِ مِلْكِهِ فَهُوَ ضَامِنٌ لِمَا يَسْقُطُ فِيهَا".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5341 - Zurah and Uthman ibn Isa narrated from Samaah, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who digs a well in his house or on his land.
Imam (as) said: "If he digs it within his own property, there is no liability upon him. But if he digs it in a public road or in a place that does not belong to him, then he is liable for anything that falls into it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5342 - In the narration of Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman from one of our companions, from Abu Abdullah (as), he was asked about bridges and whether their owners are liable for anything. Imam (as) replied: "No."
records.arabicText: 5342 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ يُونُسَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اَلرَّحْمَنِ عَنْ رَجُلٍ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ عَنِ اَلْجُسُورِ أَ يَضْمَنُ أَهْلُهَا شَيْئاً قَالَ "لاَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5342 - In the narration of Yunus ibn Abd Al-Rahman from one of our companions, from Abu Abdullah (as), he was asked about bridges and whether their owners are liable for anything.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "No."

records.chapter: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5343 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
"Whoever extends a water spout, a drain, drives a peg, ties an animal, or digs a well in the path of Muslims and it causes harm or damage to anything, then he is liable for it."
records.arabicText: 5343 - وَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "مَنْ أَخْرَجَ مِيزَاباً أَوْ كَنِيفاً أَوْ وَتَدَ وَتِداً أَوْ أَوْثَقَ دَابَّةً أَوْ حَفَرَ بِئْراً فِي طَرِيقِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ فَأَصَابَ شَيْئاً فَعَطِبَ فَهُوَ لَهُ ضَامِنٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5343 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever extends a water spout, a drain, drives a peg, ties an animal, or digs a well in the path of Muslims and it causes harm or damage to anything, then he is liable for it."

records.chapter: Chapter on Reports Regarding One Who Digs a Well or Something Else in His Own Property or in another's Property, and a Person Falls Into it and Perishes
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5344 - Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Hilal narrated from Uqbah ibn Khalid, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Among the rulings of the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) was:
'There is no liability (Jubar) for injuries caused by a mine, a well, or an animal.'"
records.arabicText: 5344 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ هِلاَلٍ عَنْ عُقْبَةَ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "كَانَ مِنْ قَضَاءِ اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ "أَنَّ اَلْمَعْدِنَ جُبَارٌ وَ اَلْبِئْرَ جُبَارٌ وَ اَلْعَجْمَاءَ جُبَارٌ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5344 - Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Hilal narrated from Uqbah ibn Khalid, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"Among the rulings of the Prophet (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) was:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'There is no liability (Jubar) for injuries caused by a mine, a well, or an animal.'"
